tavily.raw: {"query":"Ankara NATO summit 2026 €70 billion pledge Ukraine military aid details","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://cryptobriefing.com/nato-pledges-70b-military-aid-for-ukraine-at-2026-ankara-summit","title":"NATO pledges €70B military aid for Ukraine at 2026 Ankara summit","content":"NATO and Russia military clash. Kurt Volker, a prominent figure in transatlantic relations, expressed that the recent NATO summit has reinforced the unity of the alliance. According to Bloomberg Markets, Volker highlighted that the summit’s outcomes support Ukraine and convey a stronger message to Russian President Vladimir Putin. The 2026 NATO Ankara Summit, held on July 7-8, resulted in a pledge of €70 billion in military aid for Ukraine for the year 2026, with a commitment to maintain similar levels in 2027. This move underscores NATO’s ongoing support for Ukraine amid Russian occupation of 20% of Ukrainian territory and continued military advancements in key regions. * Volker’s statement suggests that the NATO summit has strengthened alliance unity, which could indicate a reduction in the likelihood of a military clash with Russia. * Market pricing has adjusted to these developments, with a slight decrease in the perceived probability of a NATO-Russia military clash by the end of 2026.","score":0.9377661,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://kyivindependent.com/nato-allies-pledge-70-billion-euros-in-defense-help-to-ukraine","title":"NATO announces 70 billion-euro Ukraine aid pledge, with much drawn from existing commitments","content":"The leaders of NATO's 32 member countries pledged 70 billion euros ($80 billion) in defense aid to Ukraine and committed to delivering the same in 2027, according to conclusions from the alliance's July 8 summit. \"For 2026, Allies pledge 70 billion euros in military equipment, assistance and training for Ukraine and affirm their sovereign commitments to sustaining at least equivalent levels in 2027,\" reads the joint declaration produced by NATO at the end of their annual meeting, this time held in the Turkish capital of Ankara. An EU and a NATO diplomat told the Kyiv Independent ahead of the summit that allies would consider the 28.3 billion euros ($32.3 billion) scheduled to be given by Brussels to Kyiv for its defense needs this year as part of the NATO pledge, even though it was agreed in April. And whereas the NATO norm is to quantify amounts in U.S. dollars, the pledge to Ukraine was expressed in euros, suggesting that bankrolling Ukraine is now a clear matter for the Europeans to handle.","score":0.9047265,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/slantchev/posts/at-the-nato-ankara-summit-the-allies-pledged-70-billion-for-ukraine-for-2026-wit/10112949843127394","title":"NATO pledges €70 billion for Ukraine's defense in 2026 ...","content":"At the NATO Ankara Summit, the Allies pledged €70 billion for Ukraine for 2026, with commitments to sustain similar levels through 2027.","score":0.90039873,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://radiomoldova.md/p/80474/nato-pledges-70b-ukraine-aid-boosts-defense-at-ankara","title":"NATO pledges €70B Ukraine aid, boosts defense at Ankara","content":"Jul 9, 2026 — Allies agreed on major new defense investments, including at least €70 billion in military support for Ukraine in 2026, with an equivalent level ...Read more","score":0.86928266,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://efe.com/english/latest-news/2026-07-08/nato-pledges-e70-billion-in-military-aid-for-ukraine-this-year","title":"NATO Pledges €70 Billion Military Aid for Ukraine","content":"Jul 8, 2026 — NATO allies on Wednesday pledged at least €70 billion in military assistance, equipment, and training for Ukraine this year","score":0.8627572,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.63,"request_id":"66b8830e-4de6-46c3-b639-28cfcbcc1a98"}
